name: convertkit-automation
description: Automate ConvertKit (Kit) tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): manage subscribers, tags, broadcasts, and broadcast stats. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: List and search subscribers with filters for status, email, and date ranges
Manage subscriber tags for audience segmentation
Unsubscribe subscribers from all email communications
List broadcasts and retrieve detailed broadcast statistics
Handle cursor-based pagination for large datasets
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 72 patterns but all are false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation-only with no executable code. Detected 'external_commands' are markdown-formatted tool names (e.g., KIT_LIST_SUBSCRIBERS), not shell execution. The hardcoded URL is the legitimate Rube MCP server endpoint. No cryptographic code or reconnaissance patterns exist.
valueStatement: Streamline your ConvertKit (Kit) email marketing workflow without manual API calls. This skill handles subscriber management, tagging, broadcasts, and analytics through Rube MCP integration.
limitations: Requires active Rube MCP connection to the Kit toolkit
Tag creation must be done through the Kit web UI, not via API
Broadcast stats are only available for sent broadcasts, not drafts
All subscriber, tag, and broadcast IDs must be numeric integers

useCases.targetUser: Marketing managers running segmented email campaigns
useCases.title: Email List Segmentation
useCases.description: Automatically tag new subscribers based on signup source or engagement level for targeted campaigns.

useCases.targetUser: Small business owners managing their own email lists
useCases.title: Subscriber Cleanup
useCases.description: Search for inactive subscribers and unsubscribe them to maintain list hygiene and reduce costs.

useCases.title: Campaign Performance Review
useCases.description: Retrieve broadcast statistics to analyze open rates and engagement metrics for past campaigns.

promptTemplates.title: Find Subscriber by Email
promptTemplates.prompt: Search for a subscriber with the email address user@example.com and show me their current status and tags.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner: Look up a specific subscriber's information

promptTemplates.title: Tag New Subscribers
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all active subscribers who joined after 2025-01-01 and tag them with tag ID 12345 for our new customer segment.
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate: Batch tagging based on signup date

promptTemplates.title: Campaign Analytics Report
promptTemplates.prompt: List all broadcasts from the last month and get the stats for each one. Summarize the open rates and identify the best performing campaign.

promptTemplates.title: Subscriber Lifecycle Management
promptTemplates.prompt: Search for inactive subscribers who haven't updated since 2024-06-01. For each one, verify their status and prepare a list for unsubscription review. Do not unsubscribe yet - just provide the list with their IDs and email addresses.
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced: Multi-step workflow with safety confirmation

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before running workflows
Use include_total_count set to 'true' (string, not boolean) when you need to track pagination progress
Verify broadcast IDs before deletion since the operation is permanent and cannot be undone
antiPatterns: Do not attempt to create tags via API - tags must be created in the Kit web UI first
Do not use boolean true for include_total_count - it must be the string 'true'
Do not unsubscribe subscribers without confirmation since it permanently removes them from all communications

faq.question: How do I set up the Rube MCP connection for Kit?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client configuration. Then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit 'kit' and follow the auth link if the connection is not active.

faq.question: Why can't I create new tags through this skill?
faq.answer: Tag creation is only available through the Kit web UI. This skill can only assign existing tags to subscribers using their numeric tag IDs.

faq.question: What happens if I unsubscribe the wrong subscriber?
faq.answer: Unsubscription is permanent for email delivery. The subscriber's data is retained but they will not receive any future emails. You would need to manually resubscribe them through the Kit web UI.

faq.question: How do I handle pagination for large subscriber lists?
faq.answer: Use the 'after' cursor value from each response as the 'after' parameter in your next request. Continue until no cursor is returned. Set include_total_count to 'true' to track progress.

faq.question: Can I get stats for draft broadcasts?
faq.answer: No, broadcast statistics are only available for sent broadcasts. Draft broadcasts do not have performance data.

faq.question: What format should I use for date filters?
faq.answer: Use YYYY-MM-DD format (for example, 2025-01-15) for created_after, created_before, updated_after, and updated_before parameters. Time components are not supported.
